name: Dehesas de la Puebla de Don Rodrigo — circular por la Sierra de Saceruela
region_intro: Jogging around Montes Norte offers diverse routes through a landscape characterized by mountainous terrain and Mediterranean vegetation. The region features hillsides covered in holm oaks, gall oaks, and aromatic plants, interspersed with plains. Protected natural areas and unique geological formations like the Volcán y Laguna de Peñarroya provide varied backdrops for running. The Guadiana River also carves through gorges, creating scenic paths for exploration. Best jogging routes around Montes Norte The most popular jogging route is Cordel de Navalrincón Trail, an 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, offering a long-distance run through the region's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra de Malagón — circular por Las Navas de Malagón, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, traversing mountainous areas with Mediterranean flora. Local runners also love the Boquerón del Estena Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through a natural monument, often completed in about 32 minutes. Jogging around Montes Norte is defined by mountainous reliefs, Mediterranean forests, and river gorges, offering both scenic beauty and varied elevations. The network provides options for easy runs, moderate challenges, and difficult long-distance trails. The routes in Montes Norte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Montes Norte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Montes Norte?
A: Montes Norte offers a diverse selection of nearly 20 running routes. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails, ensuring options for various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Montes Norte?
A: The jogging trails in Montes Norte feature a rich and varied landscape. You'll encounter mountainous terrain with varying elevations, Mediterranean forests dominated by holm oaks, gall oaks, and aromatic plants, and scenic river gorges carved by the Guadiana River. This provides both natural shade and diverse surfaces for your run.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Montes Norte?
A: Yes, Montes Norte has options suitable for beginners. An excellent easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021436072/boqueron-del-estena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón del Estena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.7 miles (4.3 km) long and can typically be completed in about 32 minutes. This trail leads through a natural monument, offering a pleasant and accessible experience.

Q: Where can I find challenging long-distance running trails in Montes Norte?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Montes Norte provides several difficult long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210339836/cordel-de-navalrincon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordel de Navalrincón Trail&lt;/a&gt; spans 11.4 miles (18.3 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211535332/sierra-de-malagon-circular-por-las-navas-de-malagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Malagón — circular por Las Navas de Malagón&lt;/a&gt; is 11.3 miles (18.2 km) with significant elevation gain, traversing mountainous areas with Mediterranean flora.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Montes Norte?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Montes Norte are designed as loops, perfect for circular runs. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211406578/sierra-de-la-cruz-hermitage-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Cruz Hermitage Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211419502/dehesas-de-la-puebla-de-don-rodrigo-circular-por-la-sierra-de-saceruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de la Puebla de Don Rodrigo — circular por la Sierra de Saceruela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while running in Montes Norte?
A: Montes Norte is rich in natural beauty. You can run past unique geological formations like the Volcán y Laguna de Peñarroya, an excellent example of a Strombolian volcano. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5634889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera de Horcajo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another stunning natural monument. The region also features protected natural areas and river gorges where you might spot diverse wildlife.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs near the running trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful water bodies. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Abraham Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6031572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Raña&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7085639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Nava de Enmedio&lt;/a&gt;, which offer picturesque settings for your runs or a refreshing stop.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Montes Norte?
A: The running routes in Montes Norte are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, scenic beauty, and the opportunity to explore protected natural areas.

Q: Can I find trails that pass through unique natural monuments?
A: Absolutely. Montes Norte is home to several natural monuments that offer unique running experiences. Besides the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021436072/boqueron-del-estena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón del Estena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; and discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant Marine Worm Tracks&lt;/a&gt;, adding a touch of geological wonder to your run.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views from summits?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy panoramic views, some routes in Montes Norte will take you to elevated points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2907392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de los Moros Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the region, offering rewarding vistas after a climb.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Montes Norte?
A: Montes Norte offers pleasant jogging conditions for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful, with Mediterranean vegetation like rockrose, heather, and aromatic plants creating a vibrant display of colors and scents. The natural shade provided by holm and gall oaks can also make summer runs more comfortable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons.

Q: Are there any facilities or recreation areas along the running routes?
A: While specific facilities vary by route, the region does have recreation areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5653127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chaparrillo Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7536315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chaparrillo Park&lt;/a&gt; are nearby points of interest that might offer amenities or a place to rest before or after your run.
tips:
Municipality located in the valley of the Tierteafuera river. In the middle of the Guadiana union basin, framed and partially divided by the Grande and Chico streams, 2 km from the Guadiana river course.
There is evidence of habitation in this term since pre-Roman times, as evidenced by the presence of several castellars, such as the one in the Castle or the one in Castilnegro, and even a motilla found in the 70s and located at the exit of the town .
There are beautiful pastures on these slopes of the mountains near the town of Puebla de Don Rodrigo. Beautiful to walk there!
